briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in fintech-banking middleware partnerships.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign European payment network, challenging the dominance of Visa and Mastercard.

- **[Robinhood launches AI stock trading, purchases on credit cards](https://financialpost.com/investing/robinhood-launches-ai-stock-trading)** — *Financial Post* — Robinhood is expanding its retail investing platform with AI-powered stock trading tools and the ability to purchase stocks using credit cards.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Wealthsimple wants to expand its banking to kids and businesses](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/wealthsimple-launching-kids-businesses-9.7207296?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian fintech leader Wealthsimple is broadening its financial services offering by launching banking products targeted at children and small businesses.

- **[Intuit lays off 17% of its global workforce](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/intuit-layoffs-2026-9.7207948?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Financial software giant Intuit, whose products are widely used by Canadian small businesses and accountants, is cutting 17% of its global staff amid restructuring.

- **[Canada's AI minister says he's more worried about creating unicorns than monopolies](https://betakit.com/canadas-ai-minister-says-hes-more-worried-about-creating-unicorns-than-monopolies/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's AI minister signalled a pro-growth stance, expressing that building globally competitive AI companies takes priority over antitrust concerns.

- **[Nasdaq spinoff sues Hiive for patent infringement](https://betakit.com/nasdaq-spinoff-sues-hiive-for-patent-infringement/)** — *BetaKit* — A Nasdaq spinoff has filed a patent infringement lawsuit against Canadian private markets fintech platform Hiive, adding legal uncertainty to the startup.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — *(Evergreen relevance)* Canada's early push to replace screen scraping with secure open banking APIs set the stage for the country's ongoing consumer-directed finance framework still being implemented today.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Upset Uber customers charged for monthly memberships they say they never signed up for](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/uber-one-membership-charges-9.7212871?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Complaints about unauthorized Uber One subscription charges raise important questions about consumer protection and transparent billing practices in embedded finance and subscription-based payment models — directly relevant to Atlantic fintech discussions around financial inclusion and consumer rights.

- **[Lastwall raises $16 million to defend critical infrastructure in cyberspace](https://betakit.com/lastwall-raises-16-million-to-defend-critical-infrastructure-in-cyberspace/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian cybersecurity firm Lastwall secured $16M to protect critical infrastructure, a timely reminder for Atlantic Canadian financial institutions and fintechs to prioritize cybersecurity as open banking and digital payments expand.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This community resource cataloguing fintech failures and harms serves as a useful reference for Atlantic Fintech ecosystem builders focused on responsible innovation, financial inclusion, and consumer trust.
